source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a bonded/welded multi-body curtain-wall frame assembly that supports a single rectangular insulating glass unit (IGU) under wind and dead loads, following the structural-glazing-tape design guide and ASTM C1184. The assembly consists of: (A) four 6063-T5 aluminum box extrusion members (75 mm box profile x 3 mm wall, two verticals and two horizontals) forming the rectangular frame; (B) four mitered fillet welds joining the frame corners; (C) one continuous high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape bead (G23F-class), 12 mm wide x 3 mm thick, applied around the IGU perimeter (~7,800 mm total bonded length); (D) one insulating glass unit (IGU) composed of 6 mm tempered outer + 16 mm argon spacer + 6 mm tempered inner (1,500 x 2,400 mm, mass ~106 kg per ASTM E2190) bonded into the frame by the glazing tape; (E) mechanical clips (>= 2) fastening the frame to the building structural slab edge.

The frame is 6063-T5 aluminum (yield 170 MPa, UTS 205 MPa, Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). The IGU mass is ~106 kg (26 kg/m^2 x 3.6 m^2). The structural-glazing-tape bond area is 12 mm width x ~7,800 mm perimeter = 93,600 mm^2 = 0.0936 m^2.

Design stress data for the high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape (G23F-class) per the structural-glazing-tape design guide: ultimate tensile adhesion 620 kPa, allowable design tensile stress 103 kPa (ASTM C1184 SF = 6 on ultimate), allowable design shear stress 70 kPa, dynamic multiplier 1.0 for static dead load and 1.3 for peak wind gust.

Design load cases: LC1 dead load = 106 kg x 9.81 = 1,040 N down, shared on lower bond line; LC2 positive wind pressure 1.5 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 5,400 N tensile into the bond; LC3 negative wind (suction) 2.4 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 8,640 N tensile; LC4 seismic in-plane shear at 0.3 g = 312 N in-plane.

Pass/fail on the assembled model: (R1) under LC3 (worst tensile case) average bond tensile <= allowable 103 kPa and <= ultimate/SF6 = 103 kPa; (R2) under LC2 with 1.3 dynamic multiplier, tensile <= 103 kPa; (R3) under LC1 + LC4 static dead + seismic shear, bond shear <= allowable 70 kPa; (R4) under LC3, aluminum frame max von Mises <= 170 / 1.65 = 103 MPa (AA ADM 2020 allowable stress design SF on 6063-T5); (R5) frame deflection perpendicular to glass plane under LC3 <= L/175 = 2400/175 = 13.7 mm; (R6) minimum structural-glazing-tape bead width 12 mm and minimum bonded contact length per ASTM C1401 shall not be reduced below 9 mm anywhere; (R_asm1) mitered corner weld DCR <= 1.0 under LC3; (R_asm2) frame-to-slab clip DCR <= 1.0 under LC3 suction.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the aluminum frame assembly with the four extrusion members, the four corner welds, the continuous bond, the IGU, and the slab clips as distinct bodies tagged with part_family and instance_index; glazing-bond layout drawing; report with per-side bond tensile/shear stress, aluminum frame max von Mises, perpendicular deflection, per-part max stress, assembly-level connection DCR, and a compliance statement vs ASTM C1184 + structural-glazing-tape allowables. The final FEM check runs on the fully assembled model only; per-part stresses are reported for visibility but do not gate pass/fail.
short_prompt:
Design a bonded/welded multi-body curtain-wall frame carrying a single rectangular insulating glass unit (IGU) under wind and dead loads per the structural-glazing-tape design guide and ASTM C1184. The frame is four 6063-T5 aluminum box extrusions (75 mm box x 3 mm wall, yield 170 MPa, UTS 205 MPa, E = 68.9 GPa, rho = 2700 kg/m^3) with mitered fillet welds at the corners; a continuous high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape bead (G23F-class), 12 mm wide x 3 mm thick, runs around the IGU perimeter (~7,800 mm, bond area 0.0936 m^2); the IGU is 6/16/6 (1,500 x 2,400 mm, ~106 kg); >= 2 mechanical clips fasten the frame to the slab edge.

Tape allowables (G23F-class / ASTM C1184 SF = 6): ultimate tensile 620 kPa; design tensile 103 kPa; design shear 70 kPa; dynamic multiplier 1.0 static, 1.3 peak wind.

Load cases.
- LC1 dead: 1,040 N on the lower bond line.
- LC2 positive wind: 1.5 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 5,400 N tensile.
- LC3 negative wind (suction): 2.4 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 8,640 N tensile.
- LC4 seismic in-plane shear at 0.3 g: 312 N.

Aluminum frame von Mises <= 170/1.65 = 103 MPa (AA ADM 2020); perpendicular deflection under LC3 <= L/175 = 13.7 mm.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: ASTM E1300 glass deflection/strength is a tabulated closed-form method.
ASCE 7-22 C_p, K_z, K_zt, and gust factor are tabulated code quantities.

prompt.title: Aluminum curtain-wall frame with high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape bonding a 1.5 x 2.4 m IGU (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Bonded/welded curtain-wall assembly: 4 6063-T5 box extrusions + 4 mitered welds + 1 continuous glazing tape bead + 1 IGU + 2+ frame-to-slab clips. Survive dead + design wind + seismic within ASTM C1184 SF 6 and AA ADM frame limits. Final FEM check on assembled model only.

parts.name: aluminum_frame_extrusion
parts.role: 6063-T5 box extrusion member forming one side of the rectangular frame

parts.name: mitered_corner_weld
parts.role: fillet weld joining two extrusion members at each corner of the rectangular frame

geometric_constraints.standard: AWS D1.2 or equivalent aluminum welding procedure

parts.name: structural_glazing_tape_bead
parts.role: continuous G23F-class acrylic-foam structural glazing tape bead bonding IGU perimeter to frame

parts.name: insulating_glass_unit
parts.role: 1,500 x 2,400 mm IGU (6 mm tempered + 16 mm argon spacer + 6 mm tempered) carried by the frame via the tape
parts.material_hint: tempered soda-lime glass per ASTM E2190

parts.name: frame_to_slab_clip
parts.role: mechanical clip fastening the frame to the building structural slab edge

geometric_constraints.function: anchor bolt or bracket clip providing fully-constrained reaction at building anchor

parts.material_hint: steel bracket per project anchor spec

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: ~1575 x 2475 x 75 mm (frame outer) with IGU inset; clip hardware extending on the building side
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Glass plane = XY, out-of-plane = Z (wind direction)

boundary_conditions.frame_to_slab_edge: mechanical clips, fully constrained at building anchor
boundary_conditions.glass_to_frame: continuous structural-glazing-tape bond, 12 x 3 mm around perimeter
boundary_conditions.inter_part_fastener_stiffness: welded corners modeled with continuous tie or weld line elements; tape bond modeled as cohesive layer with published shear/tensile allowables

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ultimate 620 kPa / SF 6 per ASTM C1184 + structural-glazing-tape design guide
170 / 1.65 per AA ADM 2020 allowable stress design for 6063-T5
L/175 with L=2400 mm per ASTM E1300 + AAMA TIR-A11
ASTM C1401 minimum retained contact width
assembly-scope weld DCR under LC3 wind suction
frame-to-slab anchor reaction utilization under LC3

requirements.governing_standard: ASTM C1184 + ASTM C1401 + ASCE 7-22 + AA ADM 2020 + structural-glazing-tape Design Guide 2023

interfaces.load_path: corner weld ties the two adjacent extrusions, closing the frame
continuous glazing tape bead transfers IGU loads into the frame extrusion inner face
tape bonded to the IGU perimeter face
mechanical clip anchors frame to building slab edge (fully constrained)

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A structural-glazing curtain wall panel is inherently an assembly of frame extrusions + corner welds + continuous adhesive bead + glass unit + building clips; monolithic modeling cannot represent the bond's shear/tensile allowable governance.
notes.speculation_caveats: Corner weld count (4) and frame-to-slab clip count (>= 2) are inferred from the prompt's 'mitered welded' and 'mechanical clips' language; the tape is modeled as a single cohesive body for convenience.
